steps.title: Split the absolute value into two equations
steps.content:
\(|16-2x|=58\) means the expression inside equals either \(58\) or \(-58\):
Case 1: \(16-2x=58\)
Case 2: \(16-2x=-58\)

steps.title: Solve each case for \(8-x\)
steps.content:
Case 1: \(16-2x=58 \Rightarrow -2x=42 \Rightarrow x=-21 \Rightarrow 8-x=8-(-21)=29\).

Case 2: \(16-2x=-58 \Rightarrow -2x=-74 \Rightarrow x=37 \Rightarrow 8-x=8-37=-29\).

The two possible values of \(8-x\) are \(29\) and \(-29\).

steps.title: Match to choices
steps.content:
• A: \(0\) — neither solution gives \(8-x=0\). Eliminated.
  • B: \(8\) — neither solution gives \(8-x=8\). Eliminated.
  • C: \(29\) — Case 1 gives \(8-x=29\). That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: \(42\) — neither solution gives \(8-x=42\). Eliminated.
